'''新山古墳'''（しんやまこふん）は、奈良県北葛城群[[広陵町]]に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後方墳]]である。
== 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その郡中でも初期に築造され、墳丘長約40メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（維持18）に盗掘を受けている。しかし、銅鏡が多数出土している。
== 副葬品 == *[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に直弧文鏡がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの埴輪にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に十二支の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *三葉文を透彫りした金銅・銀製の帯金具は、河北省定県のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）県西晋墓その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した銅製帯具と極めて類似点が高い江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものあった可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[日本の史跡一覧]]
{{japanese-history-stub}}
[[Category:古墳|しんやまこふん]] [[Category:奈良県の史跡|しんやまこふん]] [[Category:広陵町|しんやまこふん]]

== 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その郡中でも初期に築造され、墳丘長約40メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（維持18）に盗掘を受けている。しかし、銅鏡が多数出土している。
== 副葬品 == *[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に直弧文鏡がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの埴輪にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に十二支の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *三葉文を透彫りした金銅・銀製の帯金具は、河北省定県のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）県西晋墓その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した銅製帯具と極めて類似点が高い江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものあった可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。

'''新山古墳'''（しんやまこふん）は、奈良県北葛城群[[広陵町]]に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後方墳]]である。

revision.comment: テンプレート付加、広陵町のHPを参考に墳丘の数字を訂正、盗掘の表現を変える、外部リンク
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=新山古墳 |画像= |所在地=奈良県北葛城郡広陵町大塚 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|57|N|135|44|9|E|scale:10000}} |形状=前方後方墳 |規模=全長126m |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 組合式石棺 |築造年代=4世紀前半 |被葬者= |出土品=直弧文鏡、金銅製帯金具 |史跡指定= |特記事項=陵墓参考地（宮内庁指定） }} '''新山古墳'''（しんやまこふん）は、奈良県北葛城郡[[広陵町]]に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後方墳]]である。
== 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その郡中でも初期に築造され、墳丘は前方部を南にむけ、全長約126メートル、後方部幅67メートル高さ約10メートル、前方部幅約66メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（明治18年）に所有者が植樹の際に穴を掘ったところ後方部にあった竪穴式石室につきあたり、銅鏡ほか多数の副葬品が出土している。
宮内庁によって大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。
== 副葬品 == *[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に直弧文鏡がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの埴輪にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に十二支の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *三葉文を透彫りした金銅・銀製の帯金具は、河北省定県のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）県西晋墓その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した銅製帯具と極めて類似点が高い江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものあった可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[近畿の史跡一覧]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/kankotokusan/kanko/kofun.html 広陵町観光案内 古墳]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しんやま こふん}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=新山古墳 |画像= |所在地=奈良県北葛城郡広陵町大塚 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|57|N|135|44|9|E|scale:10000}} |形状=前方後方墳 |規模=全長126m |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 組合式石棺 |築造年代=4世紀前半 |被葬者= |出土品=直弧文鏡、金銅製帯金具 |史跡指定= |特記事項=陵墓参考地（宮内庁指定） }} '''新山古墳'''（しんやまこふん）は、奈良県北葛城郡[[広陵町]]に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後方墳]]である。
== 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その郡中でも初期に築造され、墳丘は前方部を南にむけ、全長約126メートル、後方部幅67メートル高さ約10メートル、前方部幅約66メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（明治18年）に所有者が植樹の際に穴を掘ったところ後方部にあった竪穴式石室につきあたり、銅鏡ほか多数の副葬品が出土している。
宮内庁によって大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。
== 副葬品 == *[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に直弧文鏡がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの埴輪にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に十二支の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *三葉文を透彫りした金銅・銀製の帯金具は、河北省定県のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）県西晋墓その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した銅製帯具と極めて類似点が高い江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものあった可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。
== 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[近畿の史跡一覧]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/kankotokusan/kanko/kofun.html 広陵町観光案内 古墳]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しんやま こふん}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町]] [[Category:前方後方墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=新山古墳 |画像= |所在地=奈良県北葛城郡広陵町大塚 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|57|N|135|44|9|E|scale:10000}} |形状=前方後方墳 |規模=全長126m |埋葬施設=竪穴式石室 組合式石棺 |築造年代=4世紀前半 |被葬者= |出土品=直弧文鏡、金銅製帯金具 |史跡指定= |特記事項=陵墓参考地（宮内庁指定） }} '''新山古墳'''（しんやまこふん）は、奈良県北葛城郡[[広陵町]]に所在する[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の[[前方後方墳]]である。
== 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その郡中でも初期に築造され、墳丘は前方部を南にむけ、全長約126メートル、後方部幅67メートル高さ約10メートル、前方部幅約66メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（明治18年）に所有者が植樹の際に穴を掘ったところ後方部にあった竪穴式石室につきあたり、銅鏡ほか多数の副葬品が出土している。
宮内庁によって大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。
== 副葬品 == *[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に直弧文鏡がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの埴輪にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に十二支の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *三葉文を透彫りした帯金具は、河北省定県43号墓（後漢中山穆王劉暢墓）のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）周氏墓群1号墓（西晋周處墓）その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した帯具と類似性が強い。江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものである可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。

== 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その郡中でも初期に築造され、墳丘は前方部を南にむけ、全長約126メートル、後方部幅67メートル高さ約10メートル、前方部幅約66メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（明治18年）に所有者が植樹の際に穴を掘ったところ後方部にあった竪穴式石室につきあたり、銅鏡ほか多数の副葬品が出土している。
宮内庁によって大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。
== 副葬品 == *[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に直弧文鏡がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの埴輪にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に十二支の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *三葉文を透彫りした帯金具は、河北省定県43号墓（後漢中山穆王劉暢墓）のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）周氏墓群1号墓（西晋周處墓）その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した帯具と類似性が強い。江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものである可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。 [[ファイル:直孤文鏡 CIMG0083.jpg|thumb|直孤文鏡（複製品）]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/kankotokusan/kanko/kofun.html 広陵町観光案内 古墳]
{{DEFAULTSORT:しんやまこふん}} {{japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=新山古墳 |画像= |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]大塚 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=新山古墳（大塚陵墓参考地）}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=墳丘長126m |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]]（内部に組合式[[石棺]]） |築造年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）第25代[[武烈天皇]] |出土品=直弧文鏡・金銅製帯金具 |史跡指定=[[宮内庁]]治定「大塚陵墓参考地」 |特記事項= }} '''新山古墳'''（しんやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]にある[[前方後方墳]]。[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の築造とされる。

実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大塚陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第25代[[武烈天皇]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。
== 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その郡中でも初期に築造され、墳丘は前方部を南にむけ、全長約126[[メートル]]、後方部幅67メートル高さ約10メートル、前方部幅約66メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（明治18年）に所有者が植樹の際に穴を掘ったところ後方部にあった[[竪穴式石室]]につきあたり、[[銅鏡]]ほか多数の[[副葬品]]が出土している。
宮内庁によって大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。
== 副葬品 == *[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に直弧文鏡がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの[[埴輪]]にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に[[十二支]]の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *三葉文を透彫りした帯金具は、河北省定県43号墓（後漢中山穆王劉暢墓）のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）周氏墓群1号墓（西晋周處墓）その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した帯具と類似性が強い。江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものである可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。 [[ファイル:直孤文鏡 CIMG0083.jpg|thumb|直孤文鏡（複製品）]]
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 広陵町観光案内 古墳]

宮内庁によって大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。
== 副葬品 == *[[1885年]]（明治18年）の発掘<ref name=kunaicho />により、[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に直弧文鏡がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの[[埴輪]]にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に[[十二支]]の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *出土した銅鏡に、9枚の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が含まれている<ref name=kunaicho>{{Cite web|url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[宮内庁]][[書陵部]] |title=主な所蔵資料／宮内庁が管理している陵墓等から出土した考古品詳細『三角縁神獣鏡』|accessdate=2018-10-14}}</ref>。 *三葉文を透彫りした帯金具は、河北省定県43号墓（後漢中山穆王劉暢墓）のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）周氏墓群1号墓（西晋周處墓）その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した帯具と類似性が強い。江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものである可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。 [[ファイル:直孤文鏡 CIMG0083.jpg|thumb|直孤文鏡（複製品）]]

宮内庁によって大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。
== 副葬品 == *[[1885年]]（明治18年）の発掘<ref name=kunaicho />により、[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に[[直弧文鏡]]がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの[[埴輪]]にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に[[十二支]]の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *出土した銅鏡に、9枚の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が含まれている<ref name=kunaicho>{{Cite web|url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[宮内庁]][[書陵部]] |title=主な所蔵資料／宮内庁が管理している陵墓等から出土した考古品詳細『三角縁神獣鏡』|accessdate=2018-10-14}}</ref>。 *三葉文を透彫りした帯金具は、河北省定県43号墓（後漢中山穆王劉暢墓）のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）周氏墓群1号墓（西晋周處墓）その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した帯具と類似性が強い。江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものである可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。
<gallery widths="190px" heights="190px"> File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|三角縁三神三獣鏡（三仏三獣鏡） 宮内庁蔵（[[東京国立博物館]]展示） File:Mirror with triangular rim and dragon and tiger design, 9 of 12, Japan, Kofun period, 200s-300s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Tokyo, Japan - DSC09181.jpg|三角縁三神三獣鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|素文縁直弧文鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） ファイル:直孤文鏡 CIMG0083.jpg|[[直弧文鏡]]（複製品、[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示） </gallery>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == *[http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 広陵町観光案内 古墳]
{{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんやまこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=新山古墳 |画像= |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]大塚 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=新山古墳（大塚陵墓参考地）}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=墳丘長126[[メートル|m]] |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]]（内部に組合式[[石棺]]） |築造年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）第25代[[武烈天皇]] |出土品=直弧文鏡・金銅製帯金具 |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「大塚陵墓参考地」 |特記事項= }} '''新山古墳'''（しんやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[古墳時代]]前期初頭の築造とされる。
実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大塚陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第25代[[武烈天皇]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。
== 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その郡中でも初期に築造され、墳丘は前方部を南にむけ、全長約126[[メートル]]、後方部幅67メートル高さ約10メートル、前方部幅約66メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（明治18年）に所有者が植樹の際に穴を掘ったところ後方部にあった[[竪穴式石室]]につきあたり、[[銅鏡]]ほか多数の[[副葬品]]が出土している。
宮内庁によって[[武烈天皇]]の大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。しかし武烈天皇の在位年と古墳の築造時期に大きな乖離があり、築造年代や立地から[[景行天皇]]朝に活動した[[葛城国造]]の宮戸彦宿禰と見る説もある<ref>[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm 沼津の高尾山古墳の保存問題]」『古樹紀之房間』、2015年。</ref>。
== 副葬品 == *[[1885年]]（明治18年）の発掘<ref name=kunaicho />により、[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に[[直弧文鏡]]がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの[[埴輪]]にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に[[十二支]]の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *出土した銅鏡に、9枚の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が含まれている<ref name=kunaicho>{{Cite web|url=http://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[宮内庁]][[書陵部]] |title=主な所蔵資料／宮内庁が管理している陵墓等から出土した考古品詳細『三角縁神獣鏡』|accessdate=2018-10-14}}</ref>。 *三葉文を透彫りした帯金具は、河北省定県43号墓（後漢中山穆王劉暢墓）のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）周氏墓群1号墓（西晋周處墓）その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した帯具と類似性が強い。江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものである可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。
<gallery widths="190px" heights="190px"> File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|三角縁三神三獣鏡（三仏三獣鏡） 宮内庁蔵（[[東京国立博物館]]展示） File:Mirror with triangular rim and dragon and tiger design, 9 of 12, Japan, Kofun period, 200s-300s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Tokyo, Japan - DSC09181.jpg|三角縁三神三獣鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|素文縁直弧文鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） ファイル:直孤文鏡 CIMG0083.jpg|[[直弧文鏡]]（複製品、[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示） </gallery>

{{日本の古墳 |名称=新山古墳 |画像=[[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後方部） |所属=[[馬見古墳群]]（南群） |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]大塚 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=新山古墳（大塚陵墓参考地）}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=墳丘長126[[メートル|m]]
高さ10m（後方部） |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]]（内部に組合式[[石棺]]） |築造年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）第25代[[武烈天皇]] |出土品=直弧文鏡含む銅鏡34面・金銅製帯金具ほか副葬品多数 |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「大塚陵墓参考地」 |特記事項= }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|大塚陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] '''新山古墳'''（しんやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大塚陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第25代[[武烈天皇]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その群中でも初期に築造され、墳丘は前方部を南にむけ、全長約126[[メートル]]、後方部幅67メートル・高さ約10メートル、前方部幅約66メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（明治18年）に所有者が植樹の際に穴を掘ったところ後方部にあった[[竪穴式石室]]につきあたり、[[銅鏡]]ほか多数の[[副葬品]]が出土している。 宮内庁によって[[武烈天皇]]の大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。しかし武烈天皇の在位年と古墳の築造時期に大きな乖離があり、築造年代や立地から[[景行天皇]]朝に活動した[[葛城国造]]の宮戸彦宿禰と見る説もある[[宝賀寿男]]「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/hitori/takaosan1.htm 沼津の高尾山古墳の保存問題]」『古樹紀之房間』、2015年。。 == 副葬品 == *[[1885年]]（明治18年）の発掘により、[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に[[直弧文鏡]]がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの[[埴輪]]にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に[[十二支]]の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *出土した銅鏡に、9枚の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が含まれている{{Cite web|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[宮内庁]][[書陵部]] |title=主な所蔵資料／宮内庁が管理している陵墓等から出土した考古品詳細『三角縁神獣鏡』|accessdate=2018-10-14}}。 *三葉文を透彫りした帯金具は、河北省定県43号墓（後漢中山穆王劉暢墓）のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）周氏墓群1号墓（西晋周處墓）その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した帯具と類似性が強い。江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものである可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。 File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|三角縁三神三獣鏡（三仏三獣鏡） 宮内庁蔵（[[東京国立博物館]]展示） File:Mirror with triangular rim and dragon and tiger design, 9 of 12, Japan, Kofun period, 200s-300s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Tokyo, Japan - DSC09181.jpg|三角縁三神三獣鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|素文縁直弧文鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） ファイル:直孤文鏡 CIMG0083.jpg|[[直弧文鏡]]（複製品、[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 観光 古墳] - 広陵町ホームページ {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんやまこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

revision.comment: 非考古学者による説の除去：被葬者の考察は慎重に行われるべきものであり、系図学の俗説が流布されるべきではない。

高さ10m（後方部） |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]]（内部に組合式[[石棺]]） |築造年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）第25代[[武烈天皇]] |出土品=直弧文鏡含む銅鏡34面・金銅製帯金具ほか副葬品多数 |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「大塚陵墓参考地」 |特記事項= }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|大塚陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] '''新山古墳'''（しんやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大塚陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第25代[[武烈天皇]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その群中でも初期に築造され、墳丘は前方部を南にむけ、全長約126[[メートル]]、後方部幅67メートル・高さ約10メートル、前方部幅約66メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（明治18年）に所有者が植樹の際に穴を掘ったところ後方部にあった[[竪穴式石室]]につきあたり、[[銅鏡]]ほか多数の[[副葬品]]が出土している。 宮内庁によって[[武烈天皇]]の大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。 == 副葬品 == *[[1885年]]（明治18年）の発掘により、[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に[[直弧文鏡]]がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの[[埴輪]]にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に[[十二支]]の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *出土した銅鏡に、9枚の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が含まれている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[宮内庁]][[書陵部]] |title=主な所蔵資料／宮内庁が管理している陵墓等から出土した考古品詳細『三角縁神獣鏡』|accessdate=2018-10-14}}。 *三葉文を透彫りした帯金具は、河北省定県43号墓（後漢中山穆王劉暢墓）のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）周氏墓群1号墓（西晋周處墓）その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した帯具と類似性が強い。江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものである可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。 File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|三角縁三神三獣鏡（三仏三獣鏡） 宮内庁蔵（[[東京国立博物館]]展示） File:Mirror with triangular rim and dragon and tiger design, 9 of 12, Japan, Kofun period, 200s-300s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Tokyo, Japan - DSC09181.jpg|三角縁三神三獣鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|素文縁直弧文鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） ファイル:直孤文鏡 CIMG0083.jpg|[[直弧文鏡]]（複製品、[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示） ファイル:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|金銅製龍文透彫帯金具（複製品、国立歴史民俗博物館展示） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 観光 古墳] - 広陵町ホームページ {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんやまこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | group = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site)}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing an assembled {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: * 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. * Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

{{Infobox Japanese burial mound | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | group = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site)}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing an assembled {{ill|stone coffin|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: * 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. * Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sarcophagus]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. * Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: ''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: ''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Pages_with_redlinks_to_fill]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: ''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | group = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site)}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: ''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: ''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | group = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site)}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: ''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:dd]] [[ja:新山古墳]]

{{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | group = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site)}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: ''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kōryō]] This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | group = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site)}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: ''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: ''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | group = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site)}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: ''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami {{Infobox {{Infobox ancient site | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | group = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site)}} | shape = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Burial Mound Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed burial mounds in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: ''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. ''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Burial Mounds|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami
