Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | group = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | construction_period = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | group = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | group = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | artifacts = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == Grave Goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major Artifacts: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | dimensions = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_other_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial_chamber = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_other_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | occupant = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | burial_other_designation = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual occupant is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:11]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Umami Kofun Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|12=draft}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=draft}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|12=draft}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|12=draft}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=draft}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|12=draft}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]]

This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|12=draft}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|12=draft}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=draft}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|12=draft}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province.
