But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|12=draft}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=draft}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|12=draft}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|12=draft}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=draft}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|12=draft}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]]

It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=draft}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|12=draft}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|12=draft}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=draft}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|12=draft}}] - Koryō Town Official Website [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Shinyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|12=draft|12=draft}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|12=draft|12=draft}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=draft|12=draft}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|12=draft|12=draft}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]]

Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: 35 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:新山古墳 up to rev 101474634
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=新山古墳 |画像=[[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後方部） |所属=[[馬見古墳群]]（南群） |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]大塚 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=新山古墳（大塚陵墓参考地）}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=墳丘長126[[メートル|m]]
高さ10m（後方部） |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]]（内部に組合式[[石棺]]） |築造年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）第25代[[武烈天皇]] |出土品=直弧文鏡含む銅鏡34面・金銅製帯金具ほか副葬品多数 |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「大塚陵墓参考地」 |特記事項= }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|大塚陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] '''新山古墳'''（しんやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大塚陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第25代[[武烈天皇]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その群中でも初期に築造され、墳丘は前方部を南にむけ、全長約126[[メートル]]、後方部幅67メートル・高さ約10メートル、前方部幅約66メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（明治18年）に所有者が植樹の際に穴を掘ったところ後方部にあった[[竪穴式石室]]につきあたり、[[銅鏡]]ほか多数の[[副葬品]]が出土している。 宮内庁によって[[武烈天皇]]の大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。 == 副葬品 == *[[1885年]]（明治18年）の発掘により、[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に[[直弧文鏡]]がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの[[埴輪]]にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に[[十二支]]の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *出土した銅鏡に、9枚の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が含まれている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[宮内庁]][[書陵部]] |title=主な所蔵資料／宮内庁が管理している陵墓等から出土した考古品詳細『三角縁神獣鏡』|accessdate=2018-10-14}}。 *三葉文を[[透彫]]した帯金具は、河北省定県43号墓（後漢中山穆王劉暢墓）のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）周氏墓群1号墓（西晋周處墓）その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した帯具と類似性が強い。江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものである可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。 File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|三角縁三神三獣鏡（三仏三獣鏡） 宮内庁蔵（[[東京国立博物館]]展示） File:Mirror with triangular rim and dragon and tiger design, 9 of 12, Japan, Kofun period, 200s-300s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Tokyo, Japan - DSC09181.jpg|三角縁三神三獣鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|素文縁直弧文鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） ファイル:直孤文鏡 CIMG0083.jpg|[[直弧文鏡]]（複製品、[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示） ファイル:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|金銅製龍文透彫帯金具（複製品、国立歴史民俗博物館展示） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 観光 古墳] - 広陵町ホームページ {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんやまこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=新山古墳 |画像=[[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後方部） |所属=[[馬見古墳群]]（南群） |所在地=[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]大塚 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=新山古墳（大塚陵墓参考地）}} |形状=[[前方後方墳]] |規模=墳丘長126[[メートル|m]]
高さ10m（後方部） |埋葬施設=[[竪穴式石室]]（内部に組合式[[石棺]]） |築造年代=[[4世紀]]前半 |被葬者=（[[宮内庁]]推定）第25代[[武烈天皇]] |出土品=直弧文鏡含む銅鏡34面・金銅製帯金具ほか副葬品多数 |陵墓=[[宮内庁]]治定「大塚陵墓参考地」 |特記事項= }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|大塚陵墓参考地 拝所}}]] '''新山古墳'''（しんやまこふん）は、[[奈良県]][[北葛城郡]][[広陵町]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後方墳]]。[[馬見古墳群]]（南群）を構成する古墳の1つ。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''大塚陵墓参考地'''」（被葬候補者：第25代[[武烈天皇]]）として[[陵墓参考地]]に治定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県西部の[[馬見古墳群]]に属し、その群中でも初期に築造され、墳丘は前方部を南にむけ、全長約126[[メートル]]、後方部幅67メートル・高さ約10メートル、前方部幅約66メートルほどの前方後方墳である。1885年（明治18年）に所有者が植樹の際に穴を掘ったところ後方部にあった[[竪穴式石室]]につきあたり、[[銅鏡]]ほか多数の[[副葬品]]が出土している。 宮内庁によって[[武烈天皇]]の大塚[[陵墓参考地]]として管理されている。 == 副葬品 == *[[1885年]]（明治18年）の発掘により、[[銅鏡]]34面出土、その内に[[直弧文鏡]]がある。この文様は3、4世紀のころに現れ、直線と弧線を組み合わせて形を作るが、その作図法は極めて難解である。各古墳の蓋・楯などの[[埴輪]]にも表現されている。銅鏡のうちの変形方格規矩神鏡は、紐の周りを方形に区画した中に[[十二支]]の漢字が、文字とは解読できないほど文様化したものになっている。 *出土した銅鏡に、9枚の[[三角縁神獣鏡]]が含まれている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[宮内庁]][[書陵部]] |title=主な所蔵資料／宮内庁が管理している陵墓等から出土した考古品詳細『三角縁神獣鏡』|accessdate=2018-10-14}}。 *三葉文を[[透彫]]した帯金具は、河北省定県43号墓（後漢中山穆王劉暢墓）のものが最も古く、2世紀に出現している。その他に盤龍文系、龍・鳳凰文系、双龍文系、龍文系などがある。その内、銀製帯具は、江蘇省宜興（ぎこう）周氏墓群1号墓（西晋周處墓）その他の六朝期の古墳から出土した帯具と類似性が強い。江南からもたらされたものか、その影響を受けたものである可能性が高い。年代的には300年前後の製作と考えられる。 File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Tumulus, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|三角縁三神三獣鏡（三仏三獣鏡） 宮内庁蔵（[[東京国立博物館]]展示） File:Mirror with triangular rim and dragon and tiger design, 9 of 12, Japan, Kofun period, 200s-300s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Tokyo, Japan - DSC09181.jpg|三角縁三神三獣鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|素文縁直弧文鏡 宮内庁蔵（東京国立博物館展示） ファイル:直孤文鏡 CIMG0083.jpg|[[直弧文鏡]]（複製品、[[国立歴史民俗博物館]]展示） ファイル:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|金銅製龍文透彫帯金具（複製品、国立歴史民俗博物館展示） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 観光 古墳] - 広陵町ホームページ {{japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しんやまこふん}} [[Category:馬見古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後方墳]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]]

Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]]

Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Museum of Japanese History|qq=}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Imperial Household Agency Reference Sites]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]]

While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Museum of Japanese History|qq=}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Shinzan Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster) | location = {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}}) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kōryō, Nara|ja|広陵町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōryō, Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher={{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=}} at {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}} File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at {{ill|National Museum of Japanese History|ja|国立歴史民俗博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Museum of Japanese History|qq=}} File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]]

The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]]

Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster) | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:陵墓参考地]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]]

In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:Candidate Imperial Mausoleums]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]]
