revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster) | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:広陵町の歴史]] [[Category:Candidate Imperial Mausoleums]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]]

Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster) | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}} located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Candidate Imperial Mausoleums]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]] {{Commons category|Shin-yama Kofun}}

In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]] {{Commons category|Shin-yama Kofun}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster) | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kōryō]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]] {{Commons category|Shin-yama Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster) | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:sekkan]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]] {{Commons category|Shin-yama Kofun}}

In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Umami Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]] {{Commons category|Shin-yama Kofun}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster) | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:馬見古墳群]] {{Commons category|Shin-yama Kofun}}

Mound (with front section on the right and rear section in the back left) | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster) | location = [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|31|56.75|N|135|44|8.16|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shin-yama Kofun (Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site)}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Shinyama Kofun]] [[ja:新山古墳]] [[sn:hinyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Shin-yama Kofun}}

It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:新山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Shin-yama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11501998}}

This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Shin-yama Kofun (新山古墳) | image = File:Shin-yama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | part_of = {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|WD=Q11668062}} | location = {{ill|Koryo|WD=Q1346830}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.532431|N|135.7356|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Total mound length: 126 meters
Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:新山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Shin-yama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11501998}}

Rear height: 10 meters | burial = {{ill|Pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (containing an assembled {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}}) | built = Early 4th century | Interred_individual = (Designated by [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]]) Emperor Buretsu (25th Emperor of Japan) | burial goods = Numerous grave goods, including 34 bronze mirrors (featuring a straight arc pattern), gilt bronze belt fittings | notes = Managed as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site" by the Imperial Household Agency | architectural_styles = {{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|WD=Q11397225}} }} [[File:Shin-yama Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site - Worship Area}}]] == Shin-yama Kofun == The '''Shin-yama Kofun''' (新山古墳) is a {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} located in [[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō, Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. It is part of the {{ill|Umami Kofun Group|ja|馬見古墳群|1=Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Umami Kofun Cluster|qq=}} (Southern Cluster). While the actual Interred_individual is unclear, the Imperial Household Agency manages it as the "Ōtsuka Kofun Reference Site," associated with Emperor Buretsu, the 25th Emperor of Japan. == Overview == Situated in the western region of Nara Prefecture, Shin-yama Kofun is among the earliest constructed Kofuns in the Umami Kofun Group. The mound faces south, with a total length of approximately 126 meters. The rear section measures 67 meters in width and 10 meters in height, while the front section is about 66 meters wide. In 1885 (Meiji 18), during tree planting by the owner, a {{ill|pit-style stone chamber|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/pit-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} in the rear section was accidentally discovered, yielding numerous grave goods, including bronze mirrors. It is now managed as a reference burial site for Emperor Buretsu by the Imperial Household Agency. == burial goods == * During the 1885 excavation: **''' 34 bronze mirrors were unearthed, including examples with a {{ill|straight arc pattern|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/straight arc pattern|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}. This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. Similar items, dating back to around the 3rd century, are known from sites in China, including 2nd-century examples in Hebei Province. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:新山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Shin-yama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11501998}}

This intricate pattern, combining straight and arc lines, dates to the 3rd–4th centuries and appears on grave goods like lids and shields in other kofun. **''' Among the mirrors, nine are {{ill|Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-shinjū-kyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/culture/shoryobu/syuzou-r05.html |publisher=[[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] {{ill|Shoryōbu|ja|書陵部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoryōbu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宮内庁書陵部|1=Archives and Mausolea Department|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346533}} |title=Major burial goods: Triangular-Rimmed Divine-Beast Mirrors from Imperial Mausolea|accessdate=2018-10-14}} * Gilt bronze belt fittings with pierced dragon patterns were also recovered. File:Mirror with Triangular Rim and Design of Buddhist Characters and Animals, Kofun period, 4th century, from Shinyama Kofun, Koryo-cho, Nara - Tokyo National Museum - DSC06346.JPG|{{ill|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|lt=Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja|三角縁三神三獣鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen-sanjin-sanjū-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]] File:Large domestic mirror, Kofun period, 300s-400s AD, bronze - Tokyo National Museum - Ueno Park, Tokyo, Japan - DSC09131.jpg|{{ill|Straight Arc Mirror|ja|直弧文鏡|1=Straight Arc Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Straight Arc Mirror|qq=}}, replica displayed at [[:en:National Museum of Japanese History|National Museum of Japanese History]] File:新山古墳出土 金銅製龍文透彫帯金具 (複製).JPG|Pierced gilt bronze belt fittings, replica displayed at the National Museum of Japanese History == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Shin-yama Kofun}} * [http://www.town.koryo.nara.jp/contents_detail.php?frmId=26 {{ill|Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja|観光 古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koryō Tourism: Kofuns|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}] - Koryō Town Official Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|新山古墳|version=101474634|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:新山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Nara prefecture]] [[Category:History of Koryo, Nara]] [[Category:Japanese imperial mausolea (unspecified)]] [[Category:Umami Kofun Cluster]] {{Commons category|Shin-yama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q11501998}}
